display_name.he: שנה של פרילנסר ישראלי: לוח שנה אסטרטגי
display_name.en: The Israeli Freelancer Year: Strategic Calendar

display_description.he: קורס בן 6 פרקים שמלמד את לוח השנה האסטרטגי של פרילנסר ישראלי: 12 דדליינים, 6 החלטות תזמון, ופרוטוקול עבודה עם רשות המסים ומע"מ.
display_description.en: A 6-chapter course teaching the Israeli freelancer's strategic calendar: 12 deadlines, 6 timing decisions, and a working protocol with Reshut HaMisim and VAT.

who_is_this_for.he: פרילנסרים ישראלים (עוסק פטור או עוסק מורשה) שרוצים להבין את לוח השנה האסטרטגי שלהם ולא רק להגיב לדדליינים.
who_is_this_for.en: Israeli freelancers (osek patur or osek murshe) who want to understand their strategic year, not just react to deadlines.

he: לזהות את 12 הדדליינים הקבועים בשנת הפרילנסר ולבנות לוח שנה אישי
להחליט בין עוסק פטור לעוסק מורשה לפי תחזית הכנסה ולא לפי ברירת מחדל
להחליט מתי להגיש תיקון מקדמות וכמה הוא חוסך לפי סעיף 187
לתזמן הוצאות והפקדות לקרן השתלמות לפי שולי המס שלך
להכין את הרבעון האחרון כדי להפחית את חשבון המס לשנה הבאה
en: Identify the 12 fixed deadlines in the freelancer year and build a personal calendar
Decide between osek patur and osek murshe based on revenue forecast, not default
Decide when to file tikun mkdamot and quantify the Section 187 savings
Time expenses and keren hishtalmut contributions to your marginal tax rate
Prepare Q4 to reduce next year's tax bill

images.alt_he: תרשים ארבעת הרבעונים של שנת הפרילנסר
images.alt_en: Four-quarter framework of the freelancer year
images.prompt: Editorial illustration: four labeled panels arranged in a 2x2 grid representing the four quarters of a freelancer year. Each panel contains a short label in clean sans-serif (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) and a tiny geometric icon symbolising its posture: closing a folder (Q1), a balance scale (Q2), a steady arrow (Q3), and a steering wheel (Q4). One single Israeli-blue accent across the icons, the rest in deep navy on warm beige paper.

Editorial illustration: a flat 12-month grid laid out horizontally, month names in clean sans-serif. Specific dates highlighted with small Israeli-blue dots (representing recurring 15th-of-month deadlines and the April 30 + January 31 + December 31 anchors). Warm beige paper background, navy text, no decorative elements, no clutter.

images.alt_en: Osek patur threshold of 122,833 ILS chart
images.prompt: Editorial illustration: a horizontal threshold line labeled with the number 122,833 (no currency symbol overlap). Below the line a small zone labeled 'osek patur', above the line a zone labeled 'osek murshe'. A few thin vertical bars cross the threshold to suggest income variability. Israeli-blue threshold line, navy text, warm beige background. Minimal — chart-on-paper feel, not a 3D infographic.

Editorial illustration: three flat document silhouettes overlapping at a slight angle, each labeled in clean sans-serif: '6111', '1301', 'מע"מ'. Document outlines in navy, one Israeli-blue accent stripe on the top document. Warm beige paper background. Minimal, no clutter, no faces, no hands.

images.alt_he: טעות נפוצה: חודש אפריל לעומת ינואר
images.alt_en: Common mistake: April surprise vs January planning
images.prompt: Editorial illustration: a single horizontal arrow timeline with two labeled markers, 'ינואר' (January) on the start side and 'אפריל' (April) on the end side. Above January, a small navy checkmark; above April, a small Israeli-blue exclamation point. Warm beige paper background. Minimal, no decorative flourishes.

images.alt_he: השוואה: דיווח מע"מ חודשי לעומת דו־חודשי
images.alt_en: VAT reporting cadence: monthly vs bi-monthly comparison
images.prompt: Editorial illustration: two side-by-side mini calendar grids. Left grid labeled 'חודשי' (monthly) with 12 small Israeli-blue dots, one per month. Right grid labeled 'דו־חודשי' (bi-monthly) with 6 larger dots on alternating even months. Flat 2D vector style on warm beige paper, navy lines, single Israeli-blue accent for the dots.

images.alt_en: Mkdamot correction timing through the year
images.prompt: Editorial illustration: a horizontal year timeline split into four quarters (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4). Two Israeli-blue downward arrows pointing at Q2 and Q3, labeled 'תיקון ראשון' (first correction) and 'תיקון שני' (second correction). Q4 marked with a small muted-gray X labeled 'מאוחר מדי' (too late). Flat 2D editorial style, warm beige paper, navy text labels.

Editorial illustration: a staircase chart climbing left to right (or right to left for RTL feel), with 5 visible steps labeled with marginal-rate percentages: 10%, 14%, 20%, 31%, 35%. The current-step indicator is a small Israeli-blue circle on the middle step. Step rises increase in height to suggest the progressive jump. Flat 2D, warm beige paper, navy step outlines, single Israeli-blue accent on the indicator.

Editorial illustration: a vertical checklist with 4 items, each a small navy-outline checkbox followed by a short label in Hebrew. Items top to bottom: 'תרומות סעיף 46', 'קרן השתלמות', 'תזמון חשבונית', 'הקדמת ציוד'. One item already checked (Israeli-blue checkmark). Flat 2D editorial style, warm beige paper, navy outlines, single Israeli-blue accent.

Editorial illustration: a 3x3 grid of stylized document silhouettes, each labeled with a short Hebrew identifier (1301, 6111, מע"מ, 106, 867, השתלמות, פנסיה, תרומות, בנק). Documents drawn as flat rounded-corner rectangles in navy outline with a small Israeli-blue accent stripe on one corner of each. Warm beige paper background. Editorial vector style, no decorative flourishes.
